bbc.title: Earthquake is devastating blow to Venezuela at time of uncertainty
bbc.description: The twin quakes come less than six months after Venezuela's then-leader Nicolás Maduro was seized by US forces.

bbc.title: People flee as building collapses on Caracas outskirts
bbc.description: The capital and its surrounding areas have seen some of the worst damage from the 7.2 and 7.5 quakes, which hit a minute apart.

bbc.title: France warns even young people's health at risk as Europe's heatwave shifts east
bbc.description: Temperatures in Germany could hit 40C in some areas while French officials say deaths linked to the heat are being seen among young people.

bbc.title: UN pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation plan after cargo ship attacked
bbc.description: The ship was reportedly struck by an "unknown projectile" near Oman but no casualties were reported.

bbc.title: Women alleging rape and sexual assault in France call to abolish statute of limitations
bbc.description: More than 50 women are calling for the abolition of the statute of limitations which they say has prevented them seeking justice.

bbc.title: Trump asks Congress for billions for Iran war, after tension with Republicans
bbc.description: But the budget faces an uphill battle as the president spars with some members of his own party over the issue.

bbc.title: Key figure in South Africa police corruption scandal pleads guilty
bbc.description: Prosecutors say Vusimusi "Cat" Matlala could provide evidence against "high-ranking officials".

bbc.title: Oil price falls back to pre-Iran war levels
bbc.description: Signs that traffic through the key Strait of Hormuz shipping route is gradually resuming has helped to push the oil price down.

bbc.title: Ryanair says it will reluctantly not charge parents to sit next to children
bbc.description: The airline had typically charged adults a fee of £8 each way to sit with their young children.

bbc.title: Oil price falls back to pre-Iran war levels

bbc.title: How you can save money on your energy bill as debts rise
bbc.description: Experts say support is available as total debt and arrears to suppliers hit a new record.

bbc.title: Apple hikes some prices by nearly 20% while Xbox raises console cost
bbc.description: Apple said it had "never seen a component price increase this much, this quickly".

bbc.title: Reeves urges Burnham to 'stick to what I'm doing' on economy
bbc.description: The chancellor throws her support behind the MP for Makerfield despite reports he could demote her if he becomes PM.

bbc.title: The legal fight to get equal pay for Germany's disabled workers
bbc.description: A test case is seeking the minimum wage for 300,000 disabled people who currently get paid less.

bbc.title: I've spent 30 years in recruitment - this is how to get a job
bbc.description: The recruitment agency boss shares his tips on getting noticed in a tougher jobs market.

bbc.title: Price cuts on family summer days out come into force
bbc.description: The government is reducing VAT from 20% to 5% on attractions and kids' meals  as schools begin to break-up.
